summary: Branch mode selected because HEAD is one commit ahead of origin/main, but the branch diff only adds a RAG context artifact and contains no Docker/workflow implementation changes. Current repository state preserves some existing image behavior, but the requested build-speed changes are not implemented: docker-publish still runs frontend:ci, frontend:ci still includes ui:build:check, pip install --no-deps . still runs after broad runtime asset copies, package-manager steps lack BuildKit cache mounts, and install_cli_tooling.sh still clears npm cache unconditionally.

requirements.description: Docker publish workflow no longer runs npm run frontend:ci when that script includes the production Vite build. Not met: .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml lines 83-88 still run npm run frontend:ci, and package.json line 27 shows frontend:ci still includes ui:build:check.

Docker publish workflow still runs frontend typecheck, lint, UI tests, and generated contract checks before image build. Partially met: the workflow still runs frontend:ci and contracts:check, so coverage exists, but it is still coupled to the duplicate production build.

Dockerfile builds frontend production assets in frontend-builder and runs tools/verify_vite_manifest.py. Met: api_service/Dockerfile lines 56-78 keep frontend-builder, npm run ui:build, and verify_vite_manifest.py.
No new frontend build artifact upload/download step is introduced. Met in current state: docker-publish only uploads/downloads digest artifacts, not frontend assets.

pip install --disable-pip-version-check --no-deps . runs immediately after copying moonmind/ and before broad non-package runtime assets. Not met: api_service/Dockerfile lines 202-213 copy moonmind plus api_service, config, docs/ReleaseNotes, .agents, and moonspec before running pip install --no-deps .
MoonSpec projection checks still run after .agents and moonspec are copied. Met: api_service/Dockerfile lines 206-212 copy .agents and moonspec before the projection checks.
npm install steps use BuildKit cache mounts. Not met: api_service/Dockerfile lines 46 and 66 run CLI npm installs and npm ci without --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm.
pip install steps use BuildKit cache mounts. Not met: api_service/Dockerfile lines 85-87, 191-198, 213, and 225-226 run pip installs without --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip.

apt install steps use BuildKit cache mounts with locked sharing. Not met: api_service/Dockerfile lines 25-29, 60, and 136-161 run apt-get installs without locked /var/cache/apt and /var/lib/apt cache mounts.

install_cli_tooling.sh no longer clears npm cache during normal Docker builds, or clearing is opt-in. Not met: api_service/docker/install_cli_tooling.sh lines 34 and 68 still run npm cache clean --force unconditionally in normal paths.
A clean Docker build of target api-runtime succeeds. Unverifiable in this read-only assessment step because long Docker verification suites/builds were explicitly out of scope.

A repeated Docker build of target api-runtime shows improved cache reuse in --progress=plain output for npm, pip, and apt-related steps. Unverifiable in this read-only assessment step because long repeated Docker builds were explicitly out of scope, and the cache mount implementation is absent.

Runtime image starts with /app/api_service/entrypoint.sh, exposes port 8000, includes workflow console static assets in both expected locations, and passes CLI verification for codex, claude, npm, and rg. Partially met by Dockerfile evidence: lines 216-217 copy static assets to both locations, lines 178-181 run CLI verification, lines 230-244 expose 8000 and set CMD, but no Docker build was run in this step.
